@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a980
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Inspector field zones must register with the inspector LayerKey — pin layer barrier with shape-snapshot TDD
+---
+## What
+
+Pressing ArrowDown at the boundary of an inspector panel lets focus escape into the window layer (board, columns, cards). The user observed: "without the focus layer the focus moves out of the inspector — the Layer is a boundary to contain navigation in the kernel."
+
+The kernel cascade enforces a layer barrier — `candidate.layer_key == layer` at `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs:499` and again at line 535 — so if focus is escaping, either:
+
+1. The inspector `<FocusLayer>` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:185`) is not actually being pushed (`spatial_push_layer` IPC missing or arriving after field zones register), or
+2. Field zones are registering with the **window** `LayerKey` instead of the **inspector** `LayerKey`.
+
+Either way, the regression-guard tests added in `01KQAXS8QKWCKFK8ENEMN7WHR1` and the unified-nav tests in `01KQCKVN140DGBCK8NF8RZM4R5` don't catch this. They mock each IPC individually and assert on call counts, but they never assert the **shape of the kernel state**: what `spatial_push_layer` / `spatial_register_zone` / `spatial_register_scope` calls fired, in what order, with what `layerKey` argument matching what the panel's `<FocusZone>` and the field's `<FocusZone>` registered with.
+
+## Approach — TDD
+
+### Step 1 — write the shape-snapshot test, watch it fail
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-focus-bridge.layer-barrier.browser.test.tsx` (new file).
+
+Mount the production provider stack with a per-test backend, dispatch `ui.inspect` to open an inspector for a task. After mount, snapshot every IPC call made during inspector mount:
+
+- The `spatial_push_layer` call for `name === "inspector"` — capture its `key` argument.
+- Every `spatial_register_zone` call for `panel:*` and `field:*` monikers — capture each one's `layerKey` argument.
+- Every `spatial_register_scope` call for the entity moniker (`task:T1`) — capture its `layerKey`.
+
+Assert:
+
+- [ ] `spatial_push_layer` for `name === "inspector"` was called exactly once and with a non-null parent (the window layer key).
+- [ ] The panel `<FocusZone>` registered with `layerKey === inspectorLayerKey` (the key from `spatial_push_layer`).
+- [ ] Every `field:*` zone registered with `layerKey === inspectorLayerKey`.
+- [ ] The entity `<FocusScope>` (if it registers — verify whether it does) registered with `layerKey === inspectorLayerKey`.
+- [ ] Order: `spatial_push_layer` for inspector fires **before** any `spatial_register_zone` for `panel:*` or `field:*` and before `spatial_register_scope` for the entity moniker.
+
+If any of these fail, that's the bug. The test pins the contract; the fix is whatever makes it pass.
+
+### Step 2 — write a kernel-state simulation test
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-focus-bridge.layer-barrier.kernel-sim.browser.test.tsx` (new file).
+
+Wire the IPC mock to a tiny in-process kernel simulator that records the registered tree exactly as the real kernel would (push layers, register zones with parent_zone + layer_key, register scopes the same way). After inspector mount, query the simulator:
+
+- [ ] `simulator.zones.filter(z => z.moniker.startsWith("field:")).every(z => z.layerKey === inspectorLayerKey)` is true.
+- [ ] `simulator.layerStack.top() === inspectorLayerKey` while the panel is open; pops back to window layer on panel close.
+
+This covers the "registration shape" question independently of the order-of-calls question in step 1.
+
+### Step 3 — write the user-visible nav-barrier test
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-focus-bridge.layer-barrier.user-flow.browser.test.tsx` (new file).
+
+Use the same kernel simulator (or a delegate that round-trips through it). Open inspector → focus the last field → dispatch `keydown { key: "ArrowDown" }`. Assert:
+
+- [ ] The kernel simulator's response (what `spatial_navigate` returned) is the **last field's own moniker** (the kernel echoed the input because no motion is possible within the inspector layer — per `01KQAW97R9XTCNR1PJAWYSKBC7`'s contract).
+- [ ] After the resulting `setFocus`, `useFocusedScope()` reports the same field's moniker (idempotent).
+- [ ] No board / column / card moniker ever appears in `useFocusedScope()` during the entire ArrowDown handling. The inspector is sealed.
+
+Symmetric: ArrowUp at the first field stays put.
+
+### Step 4 — fix whatever the failing tests expose
+
+The TDD path dictates the fix:
+
+- If `spatial_push_layer` doesn't fire for inspector → fix the conditional rendering in `inspectors-container.tsx:184-188` (currently `{hasPanels && (<FocusLayer ...>)}` — verify `hasPanels` is true at the right moment).
+- If field zones register with `windowLayerKey` instead of `inspectorLayerKey` → trace the `useCurrentLayerKey()` resolution chain. The likely culprit is a missing `<FocusLayer>` between the inspector panel content and the field zones — perhaps `<InspectorFocusBridge>` should host its own `<FocusLayer>` so per-panel content is bound by it, with the existing inspectors-container layer becoming redundant or repurposed.
+- If `spatial_push_layer` fires AFTER field-zone registers → fix mount ordering: ensure the FocusLayer's `useEffect` runs before any nested `<FocusZone>` register effects. React runs child effects before parent effects, so the layer push needs to happen synchronously at mount, not in an effect.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Shape-snapshot test (step 1) passes — inspector layer pushed exactly once, parent is window layer, all field/panel zones register with `layerKey === inspectorLayerKey`, push fires before any zone registers.
+- [ ] Kernel-state simulation test (step 2) passes — every field zone reports `layerKey === inspectorLayerKey` in the simulated registry.
+- [ ] User-flow nav-barrier test (step 3) passes — ArrowDown at last field returns same moniker, `useFocusedScope()` never moves to a non-inspector moniker.
+- [ ] Existing tests (`01KQAXS8QKWCKFK8ENEMN7WHR1`, `01KQCKVN140DGBCK8NF8RZM4R5`) still pass.
+- [ ] If a fix touches `inspectors-container.tsx`, `inspector-focus-bridge.tsx`, `focus-zone.tsx`, or `focus-layer.tsx`, the change is described in the implementation notes section of this task.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- **Strict TDD**: write the failing tests in steps 1–3 first. Watch each fail (or pass — capturing the actual current shape). Take notes on which assertion fails first; the failure narrative drives the fix.
+- Fix only what the tests expose. Do not pre-emptively rewrite the wrap chain.
+- The kernel simulator code in step 2 is a small (under 100 LOC) test helper that records the tree of pushed layers and registered zones/scopes, mirroring what the real Rust kernel does for `spatial_push_layer/pop_layer/register_zone/register_scope/unregister_scope`. Place it in `kanban-app/ui/src/test-helpers/kernel-simulator.ts` — reusable across follow-up tests for layer/zone/scope shape.
+- Cross-reference: `01KQAW97R9XTCNR1PJAWYSKBC7` (silent-None elimination — the kernel now echoes focused moniker on no-motion), `01KQAXS8QKWCKFK8ENEMN7WHR1` (regression tests that don't match production), `01KQCKVN140DGBCK8NF8RZM4R5` (unified nav — IPC routing fix).

--- original

@@ -4,83 +4,123 @@
 position_column: todo

-title: Inspector field zones must register with the inspector LayerKey — pin layer barrier with shape-snapshot TDD
+title: 'Inspector layer simplification: drop panel zone + InspectorFocusBridge; field zones at layer root; pin barrier and cross-panel nav with TDD'
 ---
 ## What

-Pressing ArrowDown at the boundary of an inspector panel lets focus escape into the window layer (board, columns, cards). The user observed: "without the focus layer the focus moves out of the inspector — the Layer is a boundary to contain navigation in the kernel."
+Simplify the inspector spatial structure to a single shared `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` containing field zones directly at the layer root. Drop the panel zone (`<FocusZone moniker="panel:type:id">`) and the entire `<InspectorFocusBridge>` component. The Layer is the only barrier needed; nothing between Layer and Field zones earns its keep.

-The kernel cascade enforces a layer barrier — `candidate.layer_key == layer` at `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs:499` and again at line 535 — so if focus is escaping, either:
+User direction:
+> "I do not think we need a 'panel zone'."
+> "I'm real skeptical about the InspectorFocusBridge purpose."
+> "One layer for the whole panel stack allowing navigation between inspectors — which you should test."
+
+## Why

-1. The inspector `<FocusLayer>` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:185`) is not actually being pushed (`spatial_push_layer` IPC missing or arriving after field zones register), or
-2. Field zones are registering with the **window** `LayerKey` instead of the **inspector** `LayerKey`.
+The panel zone wraps each `<InspectorPanel>` body in a `<FocusZone>`. Its only structural job is to be the field zones' `parent_zone` so kernel iter 0 can find peers. But if every field zone in the inspector layer registers with `parent_zone = None`, iter 0 finds the same peers (any field zone in the same layer). Drill-out at layer root echoes the focused moniker (per `01KQAW97R9XTCNR1PJAWYSKBC7`'s contract), the React `nav.drillOut` chain detects equality and dispatches `app.dismiss` — same behavior, fewer entities.

-Either way, the regression-guard tests added in `01KQAXS8QKWCKFK8ENEMN7WHR1` and the unified-nav tests in `01KQCKVN140DGBCK8NF8RZM4R5` don't catch this. They mock each IPC individually and assert on call counts, but they never assert the **shape of the kernel state**: what `spatial_push_layer` / `spatial_register_zone` / `spatial_register_scope` calls fired, in what order, with what `layerKey` argument matching what the panel's `<FocusZone>` and the field's `<FocusZone>` registered with.
+InspectorFocusBridge wraps `<EntityInspector>` in a `<FocusScope moniker={entityMoniker}>` (a leaf in the kernel's vocabulary — and an entity is conceptually a zone, not a scope) plus a CommandScopeProvider for three edit-mode commands that duplicate per-field semantics already provided by `field.edit` at the field-zone scope. Once the panel zone is gone and the inspector nav commands are gone (`01KQCKVN140DGBCK8NF8RZM4R5`), what remains is dead scaffolding.

+## Target structure
+
+```
+<FocusLayer name="inspector" parent={windowLayerKey}>   ← inspectors-container; ONE layer for all panels
+  <InspectorPanel>                                       ← per-panel chrome only
+    <SlidePanel>                                         ← purely visual, not in focus graph
+      <ClaimPanelFocusOnMount />                         ← preserved (claims focus to first field on mount)
+      <EntityInspector entity={...}>                     ← rendered directly, no FocusScope wrap
+        <Field/> <Field/> <Field/>                       ← FocusZones with parent_zone = None at layer root
+```
+
+One layer for the whole panel stack means:
+- Cross-panel nav works: ArrowLeft/Right (or beam-search by rect) moves focus between adjacent open panels because both panels' field zones share the inspector LayerKey.
+- Closing the topmost panel emits the parent layer's `last_focused`, which restores focus to whatever was focused before the panel stack opened (existing inspectors-container chain, unchanged).
+- A single panel still works: drill-out at layer root → echo → `app.dismiss`.
+
 ## Approach — TDD

-### Step 1 — write the shape-snapshot test, watch it fail
+Use `/tdd` strictly. Write failing tests first, watch them fail against the current production tree, then refactor.
+
+### Step 1 — kernel-state shape-snapshot test (new file, fails today)

-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-focus-bridge.layer-barrier.browser.test.tsx` (new file).
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-focus-bridge.layer-barrier.browser.test.tsx`

-Mount the production provider stack with a per-test backend, dispatch `ui.inspect` to open an inspector for a task. After mount, snapshot every IPC call made during inspector mount:
+Build an in-process kernel simulator under `kanban-app/ui/src/test-helpers/kernel-simulator.ts` that records every IPC call into a registry tree mirroring what the Rust kernel maintains:

-- The `spatial_push_layer` call for `name === "inspector"` — capture its `key` argument.
-- Every `spatial_register_zone` call for `panel:*` and `field:*` monikers — capture each one's `layerKey` argument.
-- Every `spatial_register_scope` call for the entity moniker (`task:T1`) — capture its `layerKey`.
+- `spatial_push_layer(key, name, parent)` → records LayerKey + parent.
+- `spatial_pop_layer(key)` → removes.
+- `spatial_register_zone(key, moniker, parentZone, layerKey, rect)` → records.
+- `spatial_register_scope(key, moniker, parentZone, layerKey, rect)` → records.

The real Rust kernel echoes the focused moniker AND emits a `focus-changed` event with that moniker per the no-silent-dropout contract (`navigate.rs` `cardinal_cascade` returns `focused_moniker.clone()` and the SpatialState emit-after-write fires). User-observable end-state is identical (focus stays put), but the IPC trace diverges from production. Future tests that count `focus-changed` events or assert on a moniker echo during a no-motion case will behave differently against the simulator vs. real kernel. Suggested fix: when `navigateInShadow` returns null, emit a synthetic `focus-changed` event with `prev_key=fromKey, next_key=fromKey, next_moniker=<focused entry's moniker>` so the simulator matches the kernel's emit-on-stay-put behavior.

+### Nits
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx:329` — Stale comment references `inspector.edit` as a scope-level shadow command, but `inspector.edit` was deleted in this card. +- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx:127` — Stale comment lists `inspector-focus-bridge` among the call sites that consume the navigate-callback compatibility shim. The bridge file was deleted in this card. Suggested fix: drop `inspector-focus-bridge` from the example list (`board-view, grid-view, app-shell` remain).
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.boundary-nav.browser.test.tsx:14-16` — Docstring claims "the React adapter's drill-out chain detects equality and dispatches `app.dismiss`," but the test exercises `ArrowDown` / `ArrowUp` keys which dispatch `nav.down` / `nav.up`, not `nav.drillOut`. The actual assertion ("moniker stays put") is correct, but the docstring conflates the boundary-nav stay-put path with the Escape-driven dismiss path. Suggested fix: trim the docstring to describe what the test actually verifies — that boundary nav at the layer edge keeps focus on the same field — and move the dismiss-via-equality discussion to a test that actually fires Escape.

+## Review Findings (2026-04-30 07:03)
+
+Re-review post-FQM (paths-as-keys) refactor. Prior findings verified resolved:
+- Kernel-simulator emit-on-stay-put: lines 291-306 of `kernel-simulator.ts` look up the focused entry and emit `focus-changed` with `prev_fq === next_fq === fromFq` when `navigateInShadow` returns null. Module docstring documents the contract (lines 47-62).
+- App-shell stale comment: `app-shell.tsx:336` reads `field.edit, card-name rename`, no `inspector.edit` reference.
+- Entity-focus-context stale comment: `entity-focus-context.tsx:177` reads `(board-view, grid-view, app-shell)`, no `inspector-focus-bridge` reference.
+- Boundary-nav docstring: tightened to describe only the stay-put path that the test exercises.
+
+Production code shape is correct: `inspectors-container.tsx` mounts a single `<FocusLayer>` (no per-panel `<FocusZone>`), `entity-inspector.tsx` no longer references `InspectorFocusBridge`/`navRef`, `field.tsx` registers `field.edit` (vim:i, cua:Enter) and `field.editEnter` (vim:Enter), `useFirstFieldFocus` captures prev focus and restores on unmount. Source-level guards in `inspectors-container.guards.node.test.ts` pin the deletions.
+
+New findings below. Two warnings (one of which is a real flaky test in the brand-new cross-panel suite) plus two nits.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.cross-panel-nav.browser.test.tsx:321-426` — The four cross-panel tests are flaky when the file is run alongside the other three new inspector test files. In an 8-run sample I observed 2 failures (~25% rate): once on `cross-panel nav respects rect y-coordinate` (line 390) and once on `ArrowLeft from a field in panel B lands on a field in panel A` (line 356); after that, 5 consecutive 14/14 passes. Solo (10/10) the file is reliable. Root cause: each test relies on a fixed-duration `await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50))` (line 345, 379, 417) plus `flushSetup`'s 50ms after fireEvent.keyDown to wait for the keydown → mockInvoke `spatial_navigate` → simulator microtask `focus-changed` → React re-render chain to settle. Under cold-browser load the chain occasionally exceeds 100ms, the focused-moniker probe is read before the focus-changed listener has updated state, and the assertion sees the source moniker instead of the navigated target. This card's stated goal is to "pin cross-panel nav with TDD" — a test that flakes ~25% of the time alongside its peers does not pin anything. Suggested fix: replace the fixed `setTimeout(50)` after each `fireEvent.keyDown` with a `waitFor(() => expect(getByTestId("focused-moniker-probe").textContent).not.toBe(<sourceMoniker>))` (or the explicit target moniker), polling until React state settles. The two boundary-nav tests in `inspector.boundary-nav.browser.test.tsx` (which assert "stays put") have the same fixed-timeout pattern but don't flake because the expected outcome is the source moniker — they short-circuit through the assertion. The cross-panel tests are the genuinely-racy ones.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/test-helpers/kernel-simulator.ts:326-331` — `findBySegment(segment)` returns the first registration whose `segment` matches and silently swallows duplicates. With the path-monikers (FQM) identity model now landed (commits af749c65e onward), `segment` is a *relative* name, not a unique identifier — the canonical key is the FQM. Two zones with the same segment in different layers are legal (e.g. two open inspector panels each with a `field:task:T1.title`, or a single task pinned in both an inspector and a side palette). The current 4 cross-panel tests use distinct task IDs (`TA`, `TB`) so segments stay unique by accident, but a future test adding two same-id panels in different windows or a task pinned in both an inspector and a side palette would silently get one zone and produce flaky results.
